relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus knows the hour and loves his own to completion.
At supper, betrayal has already entered Judas’ heart.
Knowing all is in his hands, he moves into service.
He rises, lays aside garments, and takes the towel.
The Lord kneels into water and washes feet.
Peter cannot bear the reversal of Lord washing disciple.
What is not known now will be known after.
To refuse the washing is to refuse share with him.
Peter swings from refusal to excess.
Cleansing is real, yet not all are clean.
Jesus knows betrayal even while washing feet.
After washing, he asks whether they know what has been done.
Teacher and Lord has become servant.
Those washed by the Lord must wash one another.
The act becomes example.
Servant and sent one do not outrank the source.
Blessing belongs to knowing enacted.
Scripture names the bread-sharing betrayal.
He tells beforehand so trust may awaken when it happens.
Receiving the sent one receives Jesus and the Sender.
Jesus is troubled in spirit and names betrayal.
The disciples look at one another in perplexity.
The loved disciple rests close to Jesus’ chest.
Peter gestures for the question.
The beloved asks from intimacy: who is it?
The dipped morsel is given to Judas.
After the morsel, the adversarial force enters and the act is released to happen quickly.
The table does not understand the purpose.
They misread Judas’ task as buying or almsgiving.
He receives the morsel and exits into night.
With night’s exit, glory is named.
Divine glory and Son of Humanity glory are intertwined.
Little children are prepared for absence.
The new commandment is love as he loved.
Love among them will reveal discipleship.
Peter asks where; following comes later.
Peter promises life-laying before he knows himself.
Jesus names the coming threefold denial before the rooster.
